articles.title: DISCOVERY+ NOW AVAILABLE TO SPECTRUM TV CUSTOMERS AT NO EXTRA COST
articles.description: Customers Can Unlock More Than $125/Month in Streaming App Value with Eligible Spectrum TV Plans Key Takeaways: Spectrum TV customers with eligible plans now receive ad-supported discovery+, featuring popular reality and lifestyle shows plus documentaries from HGTV, Food Network, TLC and Investigation Discovery. discovery+ expands Spectrum's Seamless Entertainment experience which brings live TV together with popular streaming apps at no additional cost. Customers without a traditional TV package can purchase discovery+ à la carte through the Spectrum App Store. STAMFORD, Conn. and NEW YORK , May 14, 2026 /PRNewswire/ -- Spectrum and Warner Bros. Discovery today announced that the discovery+ streaming app is now included at no additional cost for customers with eligible Spectrum TV plans. Now Spectrum TV customers can immediately begin streaming their favorite discovery+ hit shows, from "90 Day Fiancé" to "Gold Rush" and "Ghost Adventures." This builds on Spectrum's Seamless Entertainment experience, which brings together live TV and popular streaming apps and includes more than $125 per month in retail streaming value. Spectrum TV customers can activate ad-supported discovery+ , which retails for $5.99 per month, for no extra cost to begin streaming hit shows from HGTV, Food Network, TLC, Investigation Discovery, Animal Planet and more. Customers can also upgrade to discovery+ (Ad-Free), which retails for $9.99 per month, by ... Full story available on Benzinga.com

articles.title: ClarityCheck Announces 25M+ Total Searches Since Launch
articles.description: The platform says growth highlights evolving online behavior regarding trust, safety, and digital uncertainty. CLAYMONT, Del. , May 14, 2026 /PRNewswire/ -- ClarityCheck announces 25M+ total searches since launch. This major milestone is an indicator of how users of the platform are increasingly verifying unknown calls, profiles, and online interactions. The searches include phone numbers, emails, and images. As individuals become more concerned with scams, fake profiles, and trusting online exchanges, their behavior in the digital landscape is evolving in terms of safety and security. Why Verification Has Become Mainstream With the increased use of digital devices, consumers have become more aware of the risk of being scammed or deceived when communicating through messages, emails, and ... articles.title: Alibaba Is Morphing Into A Microsoft‐Amazon‐Nvidia Mashup As China's AI Wave Accelerates
articles.description: Alibaba Group Holding Limited (NYSE: BABA ) (NYSE: BABAF ) is starting to sound less like an e-commerce company and more like an AI infrastructure hyperscaler trying to combine the roles of Microsoft Corp (NASDAQ: MSFT ), Amazon.com, Inc. (NASDAQ: AMZN ) and Nvidia Corp (NASDAQ: NVDA ) under one roof. During its earnings call , Alibaba executives repeatedly described a business rapidly expanding across AI models, cloud infrastructure, chips, enterprise software and AI agents — all while demand for compute appears to be exploding across China. Alibaba's Full-Stack AI Push "Our full stack capabilities span models, cloud infrastructure and applications with established leadership in every layer," Alibaba CFO Toby Xu said. That transformation is already beginning to show up in the numbers. Alibaba said Cloud Intelligence Group's external revenue growth accelerated to 40%, while AI-related product revenue has now posted triple-digit growth for 11 consecutive quarters. The company added that AI-related product revenue now accounts for 30% of external cloud revenue and could surpass 50% within about a year. The strategy increasingly mirrors ... articles.title: Logitech MX Keys Mini is down to $80, a genuinely good discount
articles.description: NOW $20 OFF Logitech MX Keys Mini Wireless Keyboard View Deal I’ve been using the Logitech MX Keys S keyboard for years and I love it. If you’re looking for a low-profile keyboard that’s ideal for writing and everyday work, the Mini version is now on sale. Usually available for $100, the Logitech MX Keys Mini is now $20 off via Woot . Logitech accessories don’t often get discounts, so this is a bigger deal than it sounds. While it mimics the premium feel of the full-sized S model, the Mini drops the numpad to shrink down to a more compact 75% footprint. It keeps the essential function row, though, and tucks the arrow keys into the corner without much sacrifice. In short, that means you can centralize this board in front of you as you work for greater wrist comfort and better balance for your posture (so your mouse arm isn’t way out to the side). Battery life is no worry with this keyboard, lasting up to 10 days with backlighting enabled or a whopping 5 months with it turned off. And the included USB-C cable can charge it back up to full in just 2 to 3 hours. It has triple device pairing via Bluetooth Low Energy or the wireless dongle, and it’s easy to swap between them using the Easy-Switch keys (F1, F2, and F3). You can also customize the keys with the Logi Options+ app. Don’t get this keyboard if you need a numpad (duh), if you want full-sized arrow keys, or if you’re going to do action-packed gaming. The high wireless latency and shallow scissor switches aren’t the best for twitchy, responsive tasks like that. This is a productivity keyboard through and through, best for home office workers and students. If you’re okay with those caveats, this is a solid deal to jump on. At $80, you get the comfort and reliability of the Logitech brand, and you get free shipping if you’re a Prime member. Woot says this deal lasts another 23 days, but supplies may run out so get your order in.

articles.title: DOE, ERC demand full NGCP report on Luzon, Visayas grid disruptions
articles.description: MANILA – The National Grid Corp. of the Philippines (NGCP) has been ordered to submit a full accounting of the power supply disruptions that have affected the Luzon and the Visayas grids since Tuesday. In separate statements on Thursday, the Department of Energy (DOE) and the Energy Regulatory Commission (ERC) said the directive aims to address the incidents that have disrupted electricity supply and affected millions of consumers. “The DOE is focused on ensuring stable power supply, protecting consumers, and enforcing accountability across the power sector,” Energy Secretary Sharon Garin said. READ: More brownouts expected in Cebu as manual load [...]... Keep on reading: DOE, ERC demand full NGCP report on Luzon, Visayas grid disruptions
